title: DoorDash Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: DoorDash released its Q1 2026 earnings call transcript highlighting several key themes. The company processed 933 million orders in Q1, representing 27% year-over-year growth, with total sales reaching $4 billion. Record membership signups drove DashPass growth, with accelerating year-over-year growth in U.S. DashPass members driven by strong signup growth and reduced churn. Membership programs including DashPass, Wolt Plus, and Deliveroo Plus played a central role in frequency and retention, with more than 35 million members exiting 2025. DoorDash explicitly described DashPass as emblematic of its strategy to maximize long-term profit dollar production, while acknowledging the tradeoff that DashPass orders often generate lower contribution margin per order than non-DashPass orders on average. However, DashPass members have higher retention and order frequency, supporting faster growth and higher long-term profit dollars. Management emphasized that membership expansion supports retention and profit dollars even if margin per order compresses modestly, as subscribers order more frequently. Strong growth in DashPass membership translated to year-over-year increases in average order frequency, reaching an all-time high in Q2 2025. GAAP net income attributable to DoorDash common stockholders: $213 million, up 51% year-over-year, with Adjusted EBITDA: $780 million, up 38% year-over-year.
